All right, so the retention manager, this is a really important position as well. And you'll make this higher when you get to the point where it's very difficult based on all the challenges that you have and based on the sales that the owner is doing to focus on the retention systems and have the time and availability to run the retention systems. So that is when you bring somebody in to just run the retention systems. So what those systems are is in the retention piece of this portal, in the gym launch portal. But what the manager is doing is actually running each of those pieces. So reach outs. So they are the ones that are going to be reaching out to any clients that are not communicating. So they'll be reaching out every 14 days just to make sure that they have a pulse and to make sure that everybody is enjoying their experience with the gym if they have any questions. It's just really good to initiate the conversation with your clients. Second they'll be tracking attendance. So whatever your CRM is and printing attendance reports and tracking all of that and then reaching out based on attendance will really help your members know that you guys are invested and if they skip a workout like it doesn't go unnoticed and that they should really be focusing on getting to the gym and not making excuses and having that coach there that's dedicated to doing that is huge. Next they'll be going over handwritten cards and running that system. So imagine the feeling that your client would get if they hit like a PR at the gym and then three days later they got a card in the mail that referenced it. That can easily happen. You'll need the trainers to probably write the cards but the retention manager would be responsible for the entire system. Who writes them? When does it happen? Who stuffs the envelope? Who writes the address on it? Who puts a stamp and mails it? Who gets what? How does it all work? How do you make sure that everybody gets a card and it's not the same person every single week? They run that entire system. Events. So they'll be planning events. They'll be assisting the owner with planning events and making sure they're sign up sheets and it's posted on Facebook and the trainers are making announcements and that you rented the park or whatever your event is. They'll be doing all of that stuff and sometimes it can take a while maybe putting up streamers and decorating the gym but they'll be in charge of the events that you should be doing on a monthly basis. Then finally exit interviews. The goal of the retention manager is to retain, to keep attrition as low as possible. So they should be sitting down with every member that's looking to cancel and assessing why and maybe revisiting their initial goal. Setting a 30-day action plan to say, look, if we don't hit this goal in 30 days then we'll talk about canceling but we have a really solid plan right here. So conducting all those exit interviews and saving 25 to 50% of the people they meet with and really keeping attrition as low as possible. So the owner can do this up until a point but when the business scales and grows and the owner really needs to focus on other aspects and needs to really focus on acquisition and things like that, it really comes in handy to have somebody who's just dedicated to running these systems at your gym and making sure it's super smooth and nobody falls through the crack. That's when you need a retention manager. So how to hire this person, how to find them is in the ownership portal course and then how to train them and how to teach them exactly how it would work at your gym. That is in the downloadable course, the share funnel for ClickFunnels and you'll want to make sure that you take the time to go through that and replace all of the videos with your own videos so you're training them based on your gym. If you need the 4Rs document that is also downloadable and then this sheet right here that goes over a summary of their responsibilities is downloadable as well. So this is the retention manager. When it's time to have one, it will be a great relief and your attrition rate should lower down after you make the acquisition.
